filename: Building Safety Regulator charging scheme 2025 to 2026.pdf
title: Building Safety Regulator Charging Scheme 2025 to 2026

height_thresholds.condition: Higher-risk building (HRB) definition for residential buildings
HRB applies to buildings containing flats, care homes, hospitals

must.text: Clients must pay charges within 30 days of receiving an invoice
Accountable persons must pay HRB registration charges
Building control approvers must pay registration fees (5-year registration)
Building inspectors must pay registration fees (4-year registration)
Charges are recoverable as a civil debt if unpaid
Principal accountable person must apply for building assessment certificate within prescribed period

should.text: Applicants should raise any queries about charges within 14 days
Applicants should escalate unresolved queries to level 2 (finance team) then level 3 (senior review)

may.text: BSR may waive charges for building work solely for disabled persons
BSR may charge for additional compliance work beyond standard scope
BSR may recover additional costs for enforcement activities

key_definitions.definition: Building at least 18m high or with 7+ storeys, containing 2+ residential units, OR containing a care home or hospital regardless of height
The accountable person with overall responsibility for discharging obligations under the Building Safety Act for an occupied HRB
Person for whom building work is carried out; responsible for Gateway 2 and 3 applications
Building control approval stage before construction can commence on HRB
Completion certificate application stage for HRB before occupation
Standard charge of GBP 151 per hour for most BSR building control functions
Charge of GBP 130 per hour for BSR oversight of registered building control bodies
Certificate issued by BSR confirming HRB meets building safety standards during occupation
Reports of safety occurrences that must be submitted to BSR

exceptions.description: Building work solely for disabled persons
exceptions.condition: The building work must be carried out solely to facilitate the needs of a disabled person and must relate to their disability. Standard charges may be waived in these circumstances.
Specific provisions apply to Crown premises under separate schedules

dependencies.relationship: Primary legislation enabling the charging scheme
Primary legislation for building control functions
Statutory instrument setting charge rates and structure

dependencies.doc_ref: Higher-Risk Buildings (Key Building Information etc.) (England) Regulations 2023
Building (Registered Building Control Approvers etc.) (England) Regulations 2024
dependencies.relationship: Regulations for building control body registration
Government guidance on cost recovery principles

decision_points: Whether a building qualifies as higher-risk (affecting applicable fees)
Which gateway stage applies to the project
Whether building control approval or registered building control approver route applies
Whether major or minor revision applies to change requests
Whether regularisation route is needed for unpermitted work
Selection of appropriate building control route (BSR vs RBC)
summary_bullets: BSR charges GBP 151 per hour for most building control functions including Gateway 2 and 3 assessments
Oversight of registered building control bodies charged at GBP 130 per hour
Fixed fee of GBP 189 for building control approval applications (not HRB)
Fixed fee of GBP 113 for relaxation/dispensation applications
Gateway 3 completion certificate application costs GBP 227 fixed fee
HRB registration costs GBP 251 per higher-risk building
Building assessment certificate application costs GBP 302
Building inspector registration costs GBP 336 for 4-year period
Building control approver registration costs GBP 4,494 for 5-year period
28 schedules detail specific chargeable activities and rates
Cost recovery principle applies - those who benefit from regulation bear the cost
Payment required within 30 days; unpaid charges recoverable as civil debt
Three-level query/dispute process available for challenging charges
Exemption available for building work solely benefiting disabled persons
Scheme applies to England only under Building Safety Act 2022
Annual scheme updated from previous 2024-25 charging scheme
Includes charges for enforcement, compliance notices, and appeals
Mandatory occurrence report processing included in assessment charges
Special provisions for Crown premises and public bodies
user_impact_factors: Whether building is higher-risk (18m+/7+ storeys with residential units)
Number of higher-risk buildings in portfolio (affects total registration fees)
Whether client is applying via BSR building control or registered building control approver
Complexity of building design (affects hourly assessment time)
Whether building work is solely for disabled persons (potential exemption)
Stage of project (Gateway 2 vs Gateway 3 vs occupied building assessment)
Whether applicant is registering as building inspector or building control approver
Whether enforcement action has been taken (additional charges apply)
Whether appeals are pursued (separate charging schedule)
Whether major or minor revisions are requested during construction
confidence_notes: Exact hourly rates for specific schedules should be verified against the detailed schedules in the full document
Fixed fees quoted are as stated in the charging scheme but may be subject to annual review
The disabled persons exemption criteria may require interpretation in specific cases
Crown premises and public body provisions may have additional complexity not fully captured
Some oversight charges may vary based on investigation complexity
Document references 2024-25 scheme as predecessor but specific supersession date should be confirmed
